messages.content: Build a request-level batching layer in Effect v3 using Effect.Request + RequestResolver.makeBatched. One request type GetUser keyed by id, a resolver that batches calls into one fake DB round-trip, and a program that issues three concurrent GetUser requests that should share the same batch.
Explain briefly how Effect.Request + RequestResolver differ from a plain Effect.all, then show a typed implementation that batches GetProduct lookups keyed by sku.
What's the Effect v3 way to deduplicate concurrent requests keyed by id using Request + RequestResolver? Show a minimal example.

Write an Effect v3 batched loader for Comment records keyed by postId. Constraints: typed error channel (TaggedError NotFound), RequestResolver.makeBatched, no any, complete each request individually so partial failures don't sink the whole batch.
Build an Effect v3 memoised user lookup on top of Cache.make with a 60-second TTL, capacity 1000, and a lookup that returns User by id. Expose get(id) and invalidate(id).
What's the Effect v3 way to wrap a slow Effect<Profile, FetchError> in a TTL cache that expires after 5 minutes? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how Cache.make's TTL interacts with the lookup function, then show a typed implementation that caches weather forecasts keyed by city for 10 minutes.
Write an Effect v3 cached config loader. Constraints: Cache.make, Duration.seconds(30) TTL, capacity 64, typed error channel ConfigError, no any, expose a single load(key) effect.
Build an Effect v3 cached DNS resolver with Cache.make, 15-minute TTL and capacity 2048. Expose resolve(host) and show that a second call to the same host hits the cache.
Build an Effect v3 producer/consumer using Queue.bounded(16). The producer offers 5 integers; the consumer takes 5 and sums them. Use Effect.gen with yield* on both sides.
What's the Effect v3 way to drain every pending item out of a Queue in one shot? Show a minimal example using Queue.unbounded and Queue.takeAll.
Explain briefly when Queue.bounded backpressure matters vs Queue.unbounded, then show an Effect v3 implementation where a slow consumer naturally throttles a fast producer with Queue.bounded(2).

Write an Effect v3 program that uses Queue.bounded(8) as a work buffer. Constraints: one producer fiber enqueues jobs {id:number}, one consumer fiber takes jobs and logs via Effect.log, no any, graceful shutdown via Queue.shutdown.
Build an Effect v3 pub/sub with PubSub.bounded(8). One publisher sends three string events; two subscribers each read all three. Use Effect.scoped for subscriber lifetimes.
What's the Effect v3 way to broadcast one event to multiple concurrent subscribers using PubSub? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how PubSub differs from Queue in Effect v3, then show a typed implementation where two subscribers each receive the same event stream.

Write an Effect v3 program that implements a domain-event bus. Constraints: PubSub.unbounded, typed event union DomainEvent = UserCreated | UserDeleted, one subscriber filters to UserCreated only, no any.
Build an Effect v3 telemetry bus using PubSub.unbounded<Telemetry>. Publish three metric events; a single subscriber collects them into an array and returns it.
Build a fp-ts IOEither pipeline that reads process.env.PORT, validates it parses as an integer in [1, 65535], and returns IOEither<string, number>. Use pipe + IOE.chain.
What's the fp-ts way to wrap a throwing sync function in IOEither so the error is captured instead of escaping? Show a minimal example using IOE.tryCatch.
Explain briefly how IOEither differs from Either and TaskEither in fp-ts, then show a typed implementation that reads two sync config values and combines them.

Write an fp-ts IOEither that reads a synchronous config file path, loads it via fs.readFileSync, and parses it as JSON. Constraints: IOE.tryCatch for both side effects, pipe composition, typed error union FsError | ParseError, no any.
Build an fp-ts pipeline on a readonly array of user ids: use RA.chain to expand each id to the user's posts, RA.filterMap to drop posts without a title, and return the titles.
What's the fp-ts way to split an array into rejections and successes in one pass using RA.partitionMap? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly when to reach for RA.chain vs RA.map vs RA.filterMap in fp-ts, then show a typed implementation that applies each in one pipeline.

Write an fp-ts function summarise(inputs: readonly string[]) that returns { valid: readonly number[]; invalid: readonly string[] }. Constraints: use pipe + RA.partitionMap, no any, validator returns Either<string, number>.
Build an fp-ts pipeline that takes readonly (string | null)[] and returns readonly string[] with nulls removed and values upper-cased — use RA.filterMap + O.fromNullable.
Build an Effect v3 concurrent HTTP fetcher that caps parallel in-flight requests at 3 using Effect.makeSemaphore. Accept readonly urls: readonly string[] and return readonly Response shapes.
What's the Effect v3 way to limit concurrency to a fixed N using Effect.makeSemaphore? Show a minimal example.

Explain briefly why Effect.makeSemaphore is preferred over Effect.all({ concurrency: N }) when the bound spans multiple Effect.all calls, then show a typed implementation sharing one semaphore across two batches.
Write an Effect v3 uploader that caps concurrency at 4 using Effect.makeSemaphore. Constraints: typed error channel UploadError, no any, preserve input order in the output array.
